doctrine: Governance is not assumed. CLU keeps agentic operations inside declared scope, exposes operator-visible policy, and reports when the runtime posture drifts away from the intended command model.

documents.title: CLU Constitution
documents.summary: Defines the non-negotiable operating principles for agentic work inside Master Control Program.
documents.content: Contract before action. Scope is binding. Truthfulness is mandatory. Governance overrides convenience. Documentation is part of delivery. Compliance must be measurable. Release and operational integrity are non-negotiable.

documents.title: Change Control Policy
documents.summary: High-impact runtime changes must be deliberate, attributable, and recoverable.
documents.content: CLU expects meaningful runtime changes to be operator-visible, attributable to an approved actor, and recoverable through configuration history, exports, or reinstall paths.

documents.title: Compliance Policy
documents.summary: A change is compliant only when required conditions and evidence are present.
documents.content: Blocking violations include out-of-scope autonomy, unsafe LAN posture without explicit intent, hidden failures, missing operator confirmation for dangerous security changes, and contradictions between runtime state and declared policy.

documents.title: Documentation Policy
documents.summary: Operational understanding is part of delivery, not a postscript.
documents.content: If runtime behavior, agent authority, import/export contracts, or security posture changes, CLU expects those changes to remain explainable through surfaced policies, exports, and current governance state.

documents.title: Release Integrity Policy
documents.summary: Operational releases must preserve traceability and impact awareness.
documents.content: Installer, provider, import, and security changes should preserve version, provenance, and posture visibility so operators can understand what changed and why.

documents.title: CLU Vision
documents.summary: Provide a command-grade governance lane for local MCP and sub-agent orchestration.
documents.content: CLU exists so Master Control Program can host AI-enabled orchestration without collapsing into unchecked autonomy, undocumented drift, or opaque security tradeoffs.

roles.name: Architect
responsibilities: Define allowed scope and forbidden scope
Produce a task contract before execution

roles.name: Builder
responsibilities: Implement authorized runtime and configuration changes
Modify only approved surfaces and modules
Report what changed and any known issues

roles.name: Operator
responsibilities: Own the security and autonomy envelope
Review CLU findings and recommended actions

rules.title: No meaningful autonomous action without declared scope
rules.description: Agent autonomy should remain disabled unless the operator has explicitly enabled it for the dashboard.

rules.title: No unsafe open-LAN posture without explicit operator intent
rules.description: Security protocols should not be disabled while the browser surface remains open on the LAN.

rules.title: Troubleshooting bypass must remain visible and temporary
rules.description: Bypass posture is allowed for troubleshooting, but CLU should continue to surface it as a risk until it is turned off.

rules.title: Provider authority must align with dashboard autonomy
rules.description: Provider-level autonomous control settings should not silently conflict with the global autonomy posture.

rules.title: Imported software provenance must remain visible
rules.description: Trusted versus untrusted install history should remain visible after package, repo, or zip onboarding.

rules.title: Gateway and agent handoff material must remain exportable
rules.description: Operators should be able to export the current gateway and agent connection material from the running system.

operatorChecklist: Confirm the security posture before enabling AI autonomy.
Review trusted hosts before allowing unattended remote imports.
Treat troubleshooting bypass as temporary and reversible.
Verify exported gateway and agent profiles after provider or port changes.
